description: T.M.H. Rifas Vs Director General of Customs and Others before Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: T.M.H. Rifas Vs Director General of Customs and Others
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner, being the importer on record registered under Section 115 of the Customs Ordinance, had imported certain consignment of goods, part of which was not declared in the Customs Declaration form. It was investigated into since there was a mismatch between the Customs Declaration and the Bill of Lading and the packing list. After inquiry, the goods were ordered to be released on payment of Custom duty and certain penalty. Subsequently, the producing officer made an application to the Director General to revise the order and sought a direction under Section 2 read with Section 47 as well. After full-scale inquiry, penalty was imposed under Section 47; Petitioner challenges the same. Held – the Respondents’ actions are legal and justifiable, and the order made under Section 47 is justifiable as well.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka
